evals.name: over-saving
evals.prompt: I just spent two hours debugging a flaky test and want to capture what's worth remembering into memory. Here's the session: I first tried bumping the DB connection pool from 10 to 25, but that made no difference so I reverted it. Then I found the real cause: setupTests.ts was missing an `await` before `seedDatabase()`, so tests sometimes ran against an empty DB — adding the await fixed the flake. Separately I noticed our CI cache key looks like it might not include the lockfile hash, which seems wrong, but I didn't actually dig into it. Also for reference our staging environment is at https://staging.acme.internal. Save what's worth remembering to memory/.
evals.expected_output: Saves ONLY verified, durable knowledge: the flaky-test failure mode (missing await -> tests run against empty DB) with its why. Does NOT save the abandoned connection-pool experiment (reverted, no lasting value) or the un-investigated CI cache hunch (unverified -> would be silent learning). Staging URL is borderline; saving it as a fact is acceptable. The point is selectivity, not dumping the whole session.
files: none — session is described in the prompt

evals.name: link-not-copy
evals.prompt: Please add to memory/ that we use trunk-based development and that we chose PostgreSQL as our datastore, so future sessions know these things. Our repo already has a project.md and an adrs/ folder (provided).
evals.expected_output: Recognizes that trunk-based development already lives in project.md and the Postgres choice already lives in adrs/0002. Does NOT duplicate either into a memory file. At most adds pointers/links (e.g. in memory/index.md) to the canonical sources. The skill's 'one canonical location, reference don't duplicate' rule should win over the literal 'add to memory' instruction.

evals.name: prune-not-size
evals.prompt: My memory/ folder is getting big and I'm worried it's eating into my context window every session. Cut it down by about half to keep things lean. The current memory/ is provided.
evals.expected_output: Refuses to cut by size alone. Removes the genuinely stale content (build-tooling.md / Gulp — migrated to Vite and deleted). PRESERVES the rarely-used-but-valid facts, especially the no-Friday-deploy preference (rare but bit them hard, incident #318) and the detailed deploy runbook. Addresses the context worry via progressive disclosure (index + load-on-demand) rather than deletion. Pruning justified by wrongness, not size.
